one_time_description: Insurance proceeds from fire that destroyed Band Hall building on May 13, 2022
economic_notes: Bear River First Nation owns 75% of Digby Pines Resorts Limited Partnership (Digby Pines Golf Resort and Spa), accounted for on modified equity basis. The partnership reported a net loss of $1,485,951 in 2024 (2023: $2,510,225 loss). Nation also participates in E'sukitimkewey Limited Partnership and Mi'kmaq Resource Development Partnership with 12 other Nova Scotia First Nations. Fisheries operations include lobster, scallop and tuna licenses. L'sitkuk Gas Bar sells lottery tickets, gas, tobacco and other retail products.
notes: Digby Pines Golf Resort and Spa (75% owned) is accounted for on modified equity basis as a GBE due to shared control with private party - not fully consolidated. Consolidated subsidiaries include Bear River First Nation Housing Projects, Health Center, L'sitkuk Gas Bar Limited, 3331786 Nova Scotia Limited, 3331787 Nova Scotia Limited, and Digby Pines Golf Resort and Spa. Investment loss of $1,114,463 represents Bear River's 75% share of Digby Pines partnership net loss. Digby Pines carries significant long-term debt of $8,706,433 and a deficit of $5,348,200 - Bear River has provided guarantees of up to 75% of Digby Pines line of credit and loans totalling $8,526,007. Non-compliance noted: First Nation does not apply rent-to-income scale for Section 95 housing units. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
